observations.recommendation:
1.12 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the (a)
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their cash
records at least quarterly and adjust respective records accordingly for any
identified error; and (b) Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit BRS
on a monthly basis so that errors or discrepancies on recorded amounts
will be detected immediately and proper adjustment could be made in the
books.

2.12 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to advise the
concerned municipal officials to implement the following corrective
measures: (a) ensure that the concerned Accountable Officers immediately
liquidate all their outstanding cash advances through submission of
liquidation documents and/or refund of the unexpended balances; and that
all cash advances should be liquidated as soon as the purpose of the said
cash advances has already been served; (b) exhaust all possible legal and
corrective remedies against accountable officers such as sending demand
letters, and securing copy of death certificate and other supporting
documents in case of death of the accountable officers; and (c) refrain/stop
from granting additional cash advances to officers and employees without
first liquidating their previous cash advance/s or designate other bonded
employees with no outstanding cash advance.

3.15 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the (a)
Inventory Committee and GSO to (i) conduct physical inventory of all PPE
of the Municipality on or before January 31 of each year, conclude said
inventory count for CY 2024 and immediately submit the related reports
and documents to the Audit Team; (ii) reconcile the resultant inventory
reports with the Accounting records; (b) Municipal Accountant and GSO to
(i) maintain complete PPE records such as PPE Ledger Cards and
Property Cards for proper control and monitoring of the agency property
and equipment; and (ii) consider the one-time cleansing of PPE account
balances to improve the reliability of the accounts; and (c) GSO to issue
Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) to accountable officers to
establish accountability for issued PPE.

4.11 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a)
Municipal Budget Officer to allocate sufficient funds in the annual budget
to cover the amount of insurance premiums of all insurable assets of the
Municipality; and (b) designated GSO to (i) prepare and submit the
Property Insurance Form (PIF) in accordance with COA Circular No. 2018-
002 dated May 31, 2018; and (b) procure insurance policy to cover all
insurable assets of the Municipality.

5.7 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the (a)
Municipal Human Resources Management Officer to (i) expedite the
updating of leave records of the 193 employees of the Municipality; and (ii)
furnish the Accounting Office with the computed total money value of leave
credits of all employees that will be the basis of the latter in recording in the
books of accounts; and (b) Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and
Municipal Budget Officer to take coordinated actions to increase revenue
collections and allocate sufficient budgetary provisions to facilitate the
recognition of expense and liability accounts related to employee benefits.

6.6 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a)
Municipal Treasurer to (i) maintain an updated Real Property Tax Account
Register (RPTAR); and (ii) prepare and submit a certified list of taxpayers
and amount of taxes due thereof to the Municipal Accountant as a basis in
recording of property taxes; and (b) Municipal Accountant to recognize
RPT Receivables and SET Receivables at the beginning of each year based
on the said certified list of taxpayer and tax due submitted by the Municipal
Treasurer.

7.11 We recommended and the Management agreed to continue their efforts of
being compliant with RA 9003 also known as the “Ecological Solid Waste
Management Act of 2000”.

8.14 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the (a)
Local Development Council (LDC) through the Municipal Planning and
Development Officer (MPDO) to carefully and diligently plan the PPAs to
be funded under the 20% DF and refrain from realigning/reprogramming
funds without justifiable reasons; (b) Bids and Awards Committee, BAC
Secretariat and Municipal Engineering Office to be prudent in exercising
their functions and ensure compliance with the provisions of R.A. No. 9184
and its RIRR and COA Circular No. 2023-004 dated June 14, 2023 in all
stages of the procurement process and project implementation; and (c)
Municipal Accountant to submit the requisite documents for further
verification/validation of the transactions.

9.10 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the (a)
LDRRMO to (i) exert best efforts toward the full implementation of all
programmed PPAs to achieve better outcomes for the benefit of the
intended recipients of the programs, and to reach the goal of maximum
utilization of the LDRRMF; and (ii) provide appropriation for PPAs which
are considered necessary, doable and implementable during the year to
avoid the incurrence of unimplemented PPAs; and (b) Municipal
Accountant to continue the good performance of substantial compliance
with the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated
September 12, 2012 on the proper accounting and reporting of the
LDRRMF.

10.11 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) require the
Chairperson, GFPS-TWG to ensure compliance with Section 4.1C.7.2 of
the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC)
No.2013-01 as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 on
the review and endorsement to the DILG of the GPB and GAD AR; and
(b) strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March
18, 2014 on the timely submission of the GAD AR to the Audit Team.

11.12 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to strategize effective
measures on how to minimize, if not eliminate the incurrence of delays
in the submission of accounts;
b. Municipal Accountant to (i) require other officials concerned in the
processing and arrangement of DVs with deficiencies to effect prompt
submission thereof to her office; and (ii) ensure the submission to the
Audit Team of the paid DVs and supporting documents, Trial Balances,
and Bank Reconciliation Statements within the prescribed period.

12.8 We recommended and Management agreed to continue in exerting efforts
to (a) fully settle the remaining unremitted taxes due to the BIR amounting
to ₱7,795,534.65; and (b) strictly adhere with the timely and intact
remittance of taxes withheld as required under Revenue Memorandum
Circular No. 23-2007 and BIR Tax Revenue Regulation No. 10-2008 dated
March 23, 2007 and July 8, 2008, respectively.

13.8 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to (a) strictly adhere with the
timely and intact remittance of contributions and loan payments to the GSIS
in accordance with the provisions of R.A. No. 8291; and (b) settle
immediately the unremitted balance with the GSIS.

14.8 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to (i) adhere with the timely
and intact remittance of contributions and loan payments to the Pag-IBIG in
accordance with the provisions of R.A. No. 9679; and (ii) settle immediately
the unremitted balance with the Pag-IBIG.

15.6 We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to (i) strictly comply with the intact and
full remittance of the monthly contributions to PhilHealth, pursuant to
Section 18 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of the National
Health Insurance Act of 2013; (ii) reconcile the Municipality’s records with
PhilHealth; and (iii) settle immediately the unremitted balance of ₱570,934.40.
We commended the Management for its continuous efforts to comply with R.A. No. 9003 or the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Finance Committee / Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate the amount of ₱385,200.00 for the payment of prior year’s premiums to PhilHealth; (b) Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books the payable account to PhilHealth and make the necessary adjustments accordingly. We recommended that the Municipality’s HRM Officer ensure that the appointee is qualified for the position based on the requirements established under the Municipality’s QS Manual before the issuance of appointments to avoid invalidation/disapproval of the same. We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the (a) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records every quarter or as often as needed to ensure the correctness of the balances; and (b) Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit BRS on a monthly basis so that errors or discrepancies on recorded amounts will be detected immediately for proper adjustment in the books.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the General Services Officer (GSO) and the Municipal Accountant to (i) maintain Property Cards and Property Ledger Cards, respectively, starting with the current and recent years’ acquisitions and aim for a yearly increase of coverage until all PPE items are covered; (ii) reconcile inventory results with property records and those maintained at the Accounting Office and isolate items not reconciled for easy reference in succeeding inventory-taking; and (iii) aim to reduce the number and amount of unreconciled PPE account balances.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the (a) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to comply with the provisions on the recognition of receipts accruing to the Trust Fund; (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) comply with the accounting guidelines on the disbursement of trust funds in accordance with Sections 95 and 104 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I as amended by Sections 125, 126, 134 and 137  of the GAM for LGUs, Volume I; (ii) recognize the completed road project amounting to ₱17,949,001.00 as an addition to its Road Network Systems and Government Equity accounts in the General Fund books in accordance with Section 104 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I as amended by Section 139 of the GAM for LGUs, Volume I; (iii) facilitate return of excess fund to the Bureau of Treasury; and (iv) subject progress payments to retention money of ten percent (10%) as required in Annex E of the 2016 RIRR of RA 9184.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Treasurer to (i) maintain updated RPTAR; and (ii) prepare and submit a certified list of taxpayer and amount due thereof to the Municipal Accountant; and (b) Municipal Accountant to recognize RPT Receivables and SET Receivables based on the said certified list of taxpayer and tax due submitted by the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) cause the immediate liquidation of the cash advances granted to LGU officials/employees; (b) ensure that the liquidation reports are submitted to the Office of the COA Auditor for review, with all of the necessary documents attached therein, such as the official receipts, certificate of seminar/trainings completed, etc.; (c) exert effort in locating documents on cash advances to properly establish the responsible accountable officer; and (d) file appropriate charges against persons liable if warranted, for continuous failure to settle their accountabilities upon demand.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Chairman and Municipal Accountant to immediately submit all contract/agreements including all of its supporting documents such as bidding documents to the Audit Team for review and verification.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant to (i) recognize the procurement of relief goods, medicines and supplies for stock and distribution in the inventory account under the perpetual inventory method; (ii) maintain Supplies Ledger Card for each commodity/stock; (iii) compute the inventory cost monthly using the weighted-average method; and (iv) recognize in the books the issuance of inventory based on the submitted RSMI; and (b) Acting General Services Officer to (i) maintain Stock Cards for supplies in their custody to account for the receipt and disposition of the same; (ii) consolidate monthly the RIS for supplies and materials using the RSMI; and (iii) submit the RSMI together with the original copy of the RIS to the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Head of the BAC Secretariat to comply with the preparation and submission of the Updated/Supplemental Annual Procurement Plan in accordance with the provisions of GPPB Resolution No. 20-2015 dated July 16, 2015 and GPPB Circular 02-2020 dated May 20, 2020.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the MSWD Officer to facilitate the authentication of the applicable documentary requirements and photo copies of identification cards with specimen signatures.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the required documents immediately to the Office of the Auditor for proper/further evaluation of the claims.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the BAC Chairman to adhere to the provisions of Section 13 of the 2016 RIRR of RA 9184 requiring the BAC to invite the representative of COA and at least two observers in all stages of public bidding.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the General Services Officer to (a) strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 regarding submission of Contracts/Purchase Orders within five days from issuance thereof; and (b) notify the Audit Team on the deliveries of procured items within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Head of the BAC Secretariat to comply with the submission of the PMR within the prescribed period in accordance with the GPPB Circular 02-2020 dated May 20, 2020.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the agency officials to (a) prepare the PIF; (b) include in the agency’s annual budget the amount of premiums for the insurance covering all of its insurable assets; and (c) obtain insurance for all insurable assets of the Municipality.

We reiterated and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the (a) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their cash records at least quarterly and adjust respective records accordingly; and (b) Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit BRS on a monthly basis so that errors or discrepancies on recorded amounts will be detected immediately and proper adjustment could be made in the books.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the (a) Municipal Treasurer to be prudent in (i) cash programming in consideration of the actual collections of local revenues, from which the reasonable level of available resources for funding the obligated expenditures could be ascertained; (ii) periodic determination of expenditures limit for the essential operating needs in order reduce, if not eliminate the cash deficit; and (iii) institute effective measures and strategies to improve and maximize local revenue collections; and (b) henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, to review thoroughly the amount of estimated revenue in the formulation of annual budget so that the target receipts can be set under realistic conditions and attainable level.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant to (i) record all purchases of supplies and materials as inventory following the perpetual inventory method; (ii) maintain Supplies Ledger Card (SLC) for each commodity/stock; (iii) compute the inventory cost monthly using the moving average method; and (iv) recognize in the books the issuance of inventory based on the submitted Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI); and (b) designated General Services Officer to (i) maintain Stock Cards (SC) for supplies to account for the receipt and disposition of the same; (ii) consolidate monthly the RIS for supplies and materials using the SSMI; and (iii) submit the SSMI together with the original copy of the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) to the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Treasurer to (i) maintain an updated Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR); and (ii) prepare and submit a certified list of taxpayer and amount due thereof to the Municipal Accountant as a basis in recording of property taxes; and (b) Municipal Accountant to recognize RPT Receivable and SET Receivable based on the said certified list of taxpayer and tax due submitted by the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to (a) compute/determine the amount of impairment and provide for allowance for impairment for dormant receivables accounts as required under IPSAS 29; and (b) if conditions and circumstances on writing off the accounts would be present, initiate the procedures for the application of the request under COA Circular 2016-005.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the (a) Inventory Committee and designated GSO to (i) conduct physical inventory of all PPE of the Municipality on or before January 31 of each year, conclude said inventory count for CY 2021 and immediately submit the related reports to the Office of the Auditor; and (ii) reconcile the resultant inventory reports with the Accounting records; and (b) Municipal Accountant and GSO to (i) maintain complete PPE records such as PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards for the proper control and monitoring of the agency property and equipment; and (ii) consider the one-time cleaning of PPE account balances to improve the reliability of the accounts.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) recognize in the respective fund’s books the unrecorded transactions; and (b) regularly reconcile the balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts to enhance the reliability of the said accounts’ balances.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal BAC Chairman, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately collate the contracts/agreements and supporting documents and submit the same to the Auditor for audit purposes.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (i) terminate immediately the contracts of consultants whose services could be performed or provided by regular plantilla personnel; and (ii) exercise prudence in the procurement of services of any consultant by considering the necessity and value of the project, as well as expertise and capability requirements.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the documentary requirements prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and other pertinent issuances concerning cash advances were properly complied with; and (b) BAC to improve its compliance with the procurement law thru strict observance of the 2016 RIRR of R.A. No. 9184, particularly, Sections 48.2, 53.9 and 54.1 regarding conduct of public bidding  and Section 54.3 on the posting in the Municipality and PhilGEPS websites the award of contract to winning suppliers to promote transparency and accountability.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) allocate at least 5% of the total annual budget for the implementation of GAD-related activities as required under Section 36(a) of R.A. No. 9710 or the Magna Carta of Women; (b) require the Chairperson, GFPS-TWG to ensure compliance with Section 4.1C.7.2 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01 as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 on the review and endorsement of the DILG of the GPB and GAD AR; (c) employ the Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) tool in the attribution of costs of regular program/project to GAD; and (d) strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 on the timely submission of the GAD AR to the Audit Team.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) continue to comply with the guidelines on LDRRMF planning, utilization, and reporting; and (b) instruct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unutilized LDRRMF to the special trust fund established for said purpose.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the (a) BAC to produce a complete and detailed APP showing the milestone procurement activities of the PAPs to ensure the timely implementation of the projects; and (b) Local Development Council (LDC) through the Municipal Planning and Development Office to consider the immediate reprogramming to other priority undertakings of projects that determined to be unviable for implementation during the budget year.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (i) instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to observe the timeliness of reportorial requirements of the Commission; and (ii) direct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the periodic trial balances and financial statements to the office of the Auditor for the months of January to November 2021.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Heads of the General Services Office (GSO), Human Resource Management Office (HRMO) and Engineering Office (EO) to submit regularly the copies of Contracts and Purchase Orders together with its supporting documents within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the (a) Bids and Awards Committee and BAC Secretariat to (i) submit immediately the lacking documentary requirements for the above procurements; (ii) pursue the creation of the agency website and enhance the public disclosure of the Municipality’s procurements; and (iii) henceforth, ensure compliance with the pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its RIRR and COA Circular No. 2012-001 in all procurements through public bidding; and (b) Municipal Accountant to strengthen her review function on the requisite documentation of procurements.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to renew the appointment of the BAC and observe the requirements of Section 11.2.6 of R.A. No. 9184 and its RIRR. We also recommended and the LCE agreed to require the BAC to (a) accurately prepare the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) judiciously and meticulously with the inclusion of the required and relevant details; (b) submit the APP, including changes thereto, within the period prescribed by the Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB); (c) comply with the guidelines for the Alternative Methods of Procurement to ensure that the most advantageous price for the Government is obtained; (d) ensure that the Municipality uses the latest Philippine Bidding Documents (PBDs) and forms to be submitted by the bidders in all of its procurement activities; (e) invite observers in all stages of the procurement process; (f) prepare the Minutes of Meeting to document the BAC proceedings; and (g) prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) in the form prescribed by the GPPB and observe its submission in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to enhance its compliance on the publicity and notification of Municipality’s PPAs by: (a) submitting a list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year;  (b) submitting within ten days the notification to the Auditor that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted in the project site; and (c) requiring the Municipal  Engineering Office to (i) employ close supervision on the implementation of PPAs, and maintain a reliable mechanism on maintaining and updating the monthly reports on PPAs’ accomplishments; and (ii) closely monitor the installation of signboards/tarpaulins by the contractor.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to continue to (a) exert best efforts in implementing its COVID-19 Vaccination Program in view of the Philippine National Deployment and Vaccination Plan for COVID-19 Vaccine guidelines; and (b) intensify the information campaign to the public to spread awareness of COVID-19 vaccine benefits to achieve the herd immunity as a way to fight the said virus.

We reiterated and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the responsible officers to (a) prepare the Property Insurance Form (PIF); (b) include in the agency’s annual budget the amount of premiums for the insurance covering all its insurable assets; and (c) obtain insurance to cover all insurable assets of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to adhere with the timely and intact remittance of taxes withheld as required under Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007 and BIR Tax Revenue Regulation No. 10-2008 dated March 23, 2007 and July 8, 2008.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal HRMO/AAO to update employees’ records with the GSIS to reconcile the amount of premiums deducted with the billings for remittance thereto; and (b) Municipal Accountant to record the herein proposed adjustment to the Due to GSIS account to correct the erroneous entries previously made:

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) adhere strictly to the provision of Section 23 of Republic Act (RA) No. 9679; and (b) record the herein proposed adjustment to the Due to Pag-IBIG account to correct the erroneous entries previously made:

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to (a) cause the prompt remittance of all the contributions to PhilHealth in full in accordance with National Health Insurance Act of 2013 and PhilHealth Circular No. 1, s. 2014; and (b) reconcile the difference of the Municipal and PhilHealth balances of the Due to PhilHealth account and recognize adjustment in the account if warranted.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to (a) review and analyze the reconciling items of reciprocal accounts; (b) prepare a reconciliation report; (c) take up the necessary adjustments in the respective books of accounts.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to (a) refrain from granting additional cash advances without first liquidating the previous cash advance; and (b) ensure that the documentary requirements prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and other pertinent issuances concerning cash advances were properly complied with.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to refrain from granting cash advance to Municipal Accountant to ensure compliance with basic elements of internal control, particularly on the segregation of duties in recording and custody of assets.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to (a) stop the practice of using cash reserve for trust liabilities and financial liabilities for payment of regular expenditures to avoid the incurrence of cash deficit; (b) promptly remit trust funds due to other government agencies; (c) control disbursements based on available funds/appropriation and not on available cash balances; and (d) utilize trust fund account only for the specific expenditures for which it was created or received.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) ensure that the Municipality has a balanced budget and that enough funds are allocated for the delivery of basic and development projects; (b) instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly observe the general limitations on personal services cited under Section 325(a) of RA 7160; and (c) instruct the Municipal Accountant to justify the payment of overtime and failure to apply the general rule that the remuneration for overtime services shall be through CTO.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) invest in high impact development projects in the utilization of its 20% Development Fund so that the best possible desired level of socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes may be achieved; and (b) refrain from including/proposing projects that do not partake the nature of investment and capital expenditure.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal DRRM Officer to ensure compliance with the NDRRMC and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 by seeking the approval by the local sanggunian of the revised AIP upon recommendation of the LDRRMC to conform with the approved LDRRMP.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant and BAC Chairman to submit the required documents immediately to the Office of the Auditor for proper/further evaluation of the claims.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) instruct the MSWD Officer and Local DRRM Officer to provide information/details of the relief goods/vitamins/medicines distributied to facilitate evaluation of the implemented programs and activities of the LGU; and (b) establish the accountability of concerned LGU officials/employees in case of lapses in the relief distribution.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the MSWD Officer and Local DRRM Officer to ensure that PPAs for implementation has an approved program/plan including the selection criteria/eligibility requirements of the target beneficiaries to ensure the equitable distribution of government resources pursuant to Section 2 of PD 1445.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to require claimants to acknowledge receipt of the checks in the Check Registers as required in Section 77 of the GAM for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) allocate at least 5% of the total annual budget for the implementation of GAD-related programs, projects and activities as required under Section 36 (a) of RA 9710 or the Magna Carta of Women; and (b) require the Chairperson, GFPS-TWG to ensure compliance with Section 4.1C.7.2 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No.2013-01 as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 on the review and endorsement of the DILG of the GPB and GAD AR.

We recommended and the Management agreed to perform its function as withholding agent of the National Government and adhere with the timely and intact remittance of taxes withheld as required under Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007.
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to comply with the remittance of the mandatory deductions within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) prioritize the remittance and monitor the status of employer and employee contributions withheld to Home Development Mutual Fund; and (b) adhere strictly to the provisions of Section 23 of RA 9679.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to cause prompt remittance of all the contributions to PhilHealth in full in accordance with National Health Insurance Act of 2013 and PhilHealth Circular No. 1, s. 2014.

observations.recommendation:
1.15 We reiterated our previous recommendation on the following courses of
actions:
a. The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer should reconcile their records
every quarter or as often as needed pursuant to the above cited provisions
to closely monitor the correctness of the balances.
b. Municipal Treasurer should record the checks issued by the agency,
including cancelled checks, in chronological order for easy monitoring of
cash availability.
c. Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit BRS on a monthly basis so
that errors or discrepancies on recorded amounts will be detected
immediately for proper adjustment in the books. This will also minimize
the accumulation of unreconciled adjustments of prior years’
transactions.
d. Cause the immediate submission of the paid disbursement vouchers and
its supporting documents corresponding to the unrecorded disbursements
reported as reconciling items in the Bank Reconciliation Statements for
the General Fund, SEF and Trust Fund.

2.8. We reiterated our recommendation that management stop the practice of
using cash reserve for trust liabilities for payment of regular expenditures to avoid
the incurrence of cash overdraft and to promptly remit trust funds due to other
government agencies. Control disbursements based on available
funds/appropriation and not on available cash balances. Trust fund account should
be made available only for the specific expenditures for which it was created or
received.

3.12 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Cause the immediate liquidation of the cash advances granted to LGU
officials/employees. Cause the withholding of salaries or any money due the
officers and employees with overdue unliquidated cash advances to compel
its settlement;
b. See to it that the liquidation reports are submitted to the Office of the COA
Auditor for review. The necessary documents must be attached therein,
such as the official receipts, certificate of seminar/trainings completed, etc.
Henceforth, introduce controls that would assure that cash advances are
liquidated or reported as soon as the purpose of the cash advance is served;
c. Refrain from granting of additional cash advances to officers and employees
with unsettled cash advances to avoid the accumulation of unliquidated cash
advances;
d. Reconstruct the subsidiary ledgers for every officers and employees to
arrive at the correct unliquidated balance and to pinpoint the reason of the
abnormal balances of the employees and make the necessary adjustments, if
any; and
e. Exert effort in locating documents on cash advances to properly establish
the responsible accountable officer.
f. File the request for authority to write off unliquidated cash advances to the
COA Audit Team Leader and/or Supervising Auditor pursuant to COA
Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 if all the conditions and
requirements provided therein could be complied.

4.7. We recommended that:
The Local Chief Executive
g. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a complete Certified List of
Taxpayers, bearing the names, amount due and collectible during the
year, and submit the same to the accounting unit as the basis in setting
up of RPT/SEF tax receivables in the succeeding year;
h. Ensure that the list of taxpayers bear vital information, such as the
addresses, to facilitate, if necessary, the sending of notices and other
follow up communications relative to tax campaign activities and other
collection strategy that may be implemented by Management; and
i. Introduce collection strategy that would significantly help increase the
tax collection of the municipality.
The Accountant
a. Observe the provision under Section 20 of the New Government
Accounting (NGAS) Manual; and
b. Use the Certified List of Taxpayers and Tax Due prepared by the Office
of the Municipal Treasurer as the basis in recording the RPT/SEF tax
receivables at the beginning of the year.

5.11 We recommended the following courses of actions:
a) The immediate submission of the Reports of Actual Inventory of PPE;
b) Reconcile the result of the actual physical inventory report with the
accounting record and investigate discrepancies as required in Section
491 of GAAM Volume I;
c) Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards should be updated and properly
maintained by the GSO and Accountant/Bookkeeper, respectively, in
order to monitor the acquired assets of the municipality in compliance
with Section 43 of the NGAS Manual ; and
d) Depreciation of the acquired properties and equipment should be
properly computed and recorded in the books of the municipality to
correctly reflect the value of the assets.

6.6. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly comply with DBM
Budget Execution Guidelines No. 2004-01 regarding the grant of honoraria to
national agency officials assigned at the Municipality.

7.9 We recommended that LCE:
a. Submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the
5th day after the end of each month pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA
Circular No. 2012 – 002; and
b. Submit survey of affected areas and list of families/recipients affected by the
casualty brought by the Typhoon Karen and Lawin indicating therein the
specific relief goods distributed to support the subject transactions.

8.8 We recommended that management:
a. Prioritize the conduct of the physical inventory as required by existing
regulations and reiterated under DILG Memorandum Circular No.
2016-21 dated February 17, 2016 by following the guidelines under
Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System
(NGAS) for Local Government Units Volume 1;
b. Reconcile the results of the physical inventory with the accounting and
property records, and prepare and effect the necessary adjustments in
order to present fairly the PPE accounts in the financial statements; and
c. Furnish the Team with copy of PAR of the properties issued to outgoing
officials and demand from them its return including all other documents
and accountabilities that may be deemed necessary for the attainment of
the Municipality’s objectives and mandates.

9.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) instruct the members
of the Municipal Local School Board to prepare and cause the approval of the
annual School Board Budget for SEF by prioritizing programs, projects and
activities as stipulated in Section 1 of R.A. No. 5447, Sections 100 and 272 of R.A.
No. 7160, Deped-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01, s. 1998 dated April 14, 1998,
and No. 01-A, March 14, 2000; and (b) stop the practice of utilizing the fund for
payment of MOOE of the DepEd Office and other expenses not related to or
included in the list of prioritized expenses chargeable against SEF.

10.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Accountant to
discontinue payment of membership dues of league/federation of appointed officials.

11.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the
insurance of all government properties such as vessels and craft, motor vehicles,
machineries, permanent buildings, properties stored therein, or in buildings rented
by the Government, or properties in transit against any insurable risk with the
General Insurance Fund by the GSIS.

12.4 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. secure the approved copy of the Ten-Year Solid Waste Management
Plan;
b. avail of the fund set aside as Solid Waste Management Fund in the
National Treasury pursuant to Section 46 of R.A. No. 9003, upon receipt
of the approved copy of the plan;
c. strictly enforce implementation of the ordinances pertaining to solid
waste management and other relevant policies and intensify information
campaign on the mandatory segregation at source, “no segregation, no
collection” policy and “no-littering” policy;
d. through the Liga ng mga Barangay, encourage active participation of
barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the
government by educating their respective constituents on the proper
segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes;
e. comply with the requirements of the law on waste segregation by
collecting only those garbage that are properly segregated;
f. provide facility for composting of biodegradable wastes; and
g. strictly enforce the establishment/use of Material Recovery Facility in
barangay levels.

13.9 We recommended that the Members of the SB consider enacting appropriate
restrictions in the operation of three-wheeled public conveyance thru creation of
ordinances to prevent undesirable outcomes as a result of unregulated operation of
tricycles along the national highways within the municipality, which may further
pose hazards and imminent danger to pedestrians and motorists’ lives and
properties.

14.7 We recommended that the LCE direct the GSO to procure its common-use
supplies from the PS – DBM in compliance with Executive Order 17 and Section
53(5) of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184. In case of non-availability of items in the
PS-DBM, we recommend that procurement of these common use goods should be
procured in bulk quantities to obtain the most advantageous price.

15.6 We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit
the monthly monitoring report of the project status and inform the audit team of the
posting of project signboards and/or public notices within the time specified in the
aforementioned circular for validation.

16.11 We reiterated our earlier recommendation that Management instruct the
concerned officials, particularly the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman and the
Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2009-001 requiring
the submission of Purchase Orders/contacts and supporting documents within five
(5) days after issuance/perfection to the Office of the Auditor to ensure timely
review/evaluation of the contracts and inspection of deliveries, respectively.

17.10 We recommended that the Municipality perform its function as withholding
agent of the National Government and adhere with the timely and intact remittance
of taxes withheld as required under Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007
dated March 20, 2007. We also recommended that the Municipality exert effort in
reconciling the undetermined amount of Due to BIR balance as of December 31,
2016 and cause the remittance thereof.

18.6 We recommended that the Municipality remit all compulsory contributions
withheld promptly to the GSIS.

19.2 We recommended that management (a) enforce the immediate settlement of
the remaining disallowances; and (b) the strict adherence to the accounting and
auditing rules and regulations in the processing and documentation of transactions
to avoid further suspension or disallowance.
